concepts.name: Experiment
concepts.short_definition: A process with uncertain outcomes.

concepts.name: Outcome
concepts.short_definition: A single possible result of an experiment.

concepts.name: Sample Space
concepts.short_definition: The set of all possible outcomes of an experiment.

concepts.name: Event
concepts.short_definition: A subset of the sample space, representing a specific outcome or set of outcomes.

concepts.name: Certain Event
concepts.short_definition: An event that is guaranteed to occur.

concepts.name: Impossible Event
concepts.short_definition: An event that cannot occur.

concepts.name: Simple Event
concepts.short_definition: An event consisting of a single outcome.

concepts.name: Compound Event
concepts.short_definition: An event consisting of more than one outcome.

concepts.name: Mutually Exclusive Events
concepts.short_definition: Events that cannot occur at the same time.

concepts.name: Independent Events
concepts.short_definition: Events where the occurrence of one does not affect the probability of the other.

concepts.name: Dependent Events
concepts.short_definition: Events where the occurrence of one affects the probability of the other.

concepts.name: Probability
concepts.short_definition: A measure of how likely an event is to occur.

concepts.name: Theoretical Probability
concepts.short_definition: Probability calculated based on equally likely outcomes.

concepts.name: Empirical Probability
concepts.short_definition: Probability calculated based on observed frequencies from an experiment.

concepts.name: Subjective Probability
concepts.short_definition: Probability based on personal belief or judgment.

concepts.name: Probability Scale
concepts.short_definition: A scale from 0 to 1 representing the likelihood of an event.

concepts.name: Probability of Zero
concepts.short_definition: Represents an impossible event.

concepts.name: Probability of One
concepts.short_definition: Represents a certain event.

concepts.name: Complementary Events
concepts.short_definition: Two events where one occurs if and only if the other does not.

concepts.name: Union of Events
concepts.short_definition: The event that at least one of two events occurs.

concepts.name: Intersection of Events
concepts.short_definition: The event that both of two events occur.

concepts.name: Addition Rule
concepts.short_definition: Formula for calculating the probability of the union of two events.

concepts.name: Multiplication Rule
concepts.short_definition: Formula for calculating the probability of the intersection of two events.

concepts.name: Conditional Probability
concepts.short_definition: The probability of an event occurring given that another event has already occurred.

concepts.name: Law of Total Probability
concepts.short_definition: A rule for calculating the probability of an event by summing probabilities over a partition of the sample space.

concepts.name: Bayes' Theorem
concepts.short_definition: A theorem relating conditional probabilities of events.

concepts.name: Frequency Table
concepts.short_definition: A table that displays the frequency of different outcomes.

concepts.name: Relative Frequency Table
concepts.short_definition: A table showing the proportion of times each outcome occurs.

concepts.name: Tree Diagram
concepts.short_definition: A visual tool to represent sequential events and their probabilities.

concepts.name: Venn Diagram
concepts.short_definition: A diagram used to show relationships between sets, often used for events.

concepts.name: Fair Coin
concepts.short_definition: A coin with an equal probability of landing on heads or tails.

concepts.name: Fair Die
concepts.short_definition: A die with an equal probability of landing on any of its faces.

concepts.name: Random Variable
concepts.short_definition: A variable whose value is a numerical outcome of a random phenomenon.

concepts.name: Discrete Random Variable
concepts.short_definition: A random variable that can only take a finite number of values or a countably infinite number of values.

concepts.name: Continuous Random Variable
concepts.short_definition: A random variable that can take any value within a given range.

concepts.name: Probability Distribution
concepts.short_definition: A function that describes the likelihood of obtaining the possible values that a random variable can assume.

concepts.name: Expected Value
concepts.short_definition: The weighted average of all possible values of a random variable.

concepts.name: Law of Large Numbers
concepts.short_definition: As the number of trials increases, the empirical probability approaches the theoretical probability.

concepts.name: Bernoulli Trial
concepts.short_definition: An experiment with only two possible outcomes, success or failure.
